text: The burden<S>4853</S> of Nineveh<S>5210</S>. The book<S>5612</S> of the vision<S>2377</S> of Nahum<S>5151</S> the Elkoshite<S>512</S>.

Jehovah<S>3068</S> is a jealous<S>7072</S> God<S>410</S> and avengeth<S>5358</S>; Jehovah<S>3068</S> avengeth<S>5358</S> and is full of wrath<S>1167</S>; Jehovah<S>3068</S> taketh vengeance<S>5358</S> on his adversaries<S>6862</S>, and he reserveth<S>5201</S> wrath for his enemies<S>341</S>.

Jehovah<S>3068</S> is slow<S>750</S> to anger<S>639</S>, and great<S>1419</S> in power<S>3581</S>, and will by no means<S>5352</S> clear<S>5352</S> the guilty<S>3068</S>: Jehovah hath his way<S>1870</S> in the whirlwind<S>5492</S> and in the storm<S>8183</S>, and the clouds<S>6051</S> are the dust<S>80</S> of his feet<S>7272</S>.

He rebuketh<S>1605</S> the sea<S>3220</S>, and maketh it dry<S>2717</S>, and drieth up<S>3001</S> all the rivers<S>5104</S>: Bashan<S>1316</S> languisheth<S>535</S>, and Carmel<S>3760</S>; and the flower<S>6525</S> of Lebanon<S>3844</S> languisheth<S>535</S>.

The mountains<S>2022</S> quake<S>7493</S> at him<S>1389</S>, and the hills melt<S>4127</S>; and the earth<S>776</S> is upheaved<S>5375</S> at his presence<S>6440</S>, yea<S>8398</S>, the world, and all that dwell therein<S>3427</S>.

Who can stand<S>5975</S> before<S>6440</S> his indignation<S>2195</S>? and who can abide<S>6965</S> in the fierceness<S>2740</S> of his anger<S>639</S>? his wrath<S>2534</S> is poured out<S>5413</S> like fire<S>784</S>, and the rocks<S>6697</S> are broken asunder by him<S>5422</S>.
Jehovah<S>3068</S> is good<S>2896</S>, a stronghold<S>4581</S> in the day<S>3117</S> of trouble<S>6869</S>; and he knoweth<S>3045</S> them that take refuge in him<S>2620</S>.
But with an over-running<S>5674</S> flood<S>7858</S> he will make<S>6213</S> a full end<S>3617</S> of her place<S>4725</S>, and will pursue<S>7291</S> his enemies<S>341</S> into darkness<S>2822</S>.
What do ye devise<S>2803</S> against Jehovah<S>3068</S>? he will make<S>6213</S> a full end<S>3617</S>; affliction<S>6869</S> shall not rise up<S>6965</S> the second time<S>6471</S>.
For entangled<S>5440</S> like thorns<S>5518</S>, and drunken<S>5433</S> as with their drink<S>5435</S>, they are consumed<S>398</S> utterly<S>4392</S> as dry<S>3002</S> stubble<S>7179</S>.
There is one gone forth out<S>3318</S> of thee<S>2803</S>, that deviseth evil<S>7451</S> against Jehovah<S>3068</S>, that counselleth<S>3289</S> wickedness<S>1100</S>.

Thus saith<S>559</S> Jehovah<S>3068</S>: Though they be in full strength<S>8003</S>, and likewise many<S>7227</S>, even so shall they be cut down<S>1494</S>, and he shall pass away<S>5674</S>. Though I have afflicted<S>6031</S> thee<S>6031</S>, I will afflict thee no more.
And now will I break<S>7665</S> his yoke<S>4132</S> from off thee<S>5423</S>, and will burst thy bonds<S>4147</S> in sunder<S>5423</S>.

And Jehovah<S>3068</S> hath given commandment<S>6680</S> concerning thee<S>8034</S>, that no more of thy name be sown<S>2232</S>: out of the house<S>1004</S> of thy gods<S>430</S> will I cut off<S>3772</S> the graven image<S>6459</S> and the molten image<S>4541</S>; I will make<S>7760</S> thy grave<S>6913</S>; for thou art vile<S>7043</S>.

Behold<S>2022</S>, upon the mountains the feet<S>7272</S> of him that bringeth good tidings<S>1319</S>, that publisheth<S>8085</S> peace<S>7965</S>! Keep<S>2287</S> thy feasts<S>2282</S>, O Judah<S>3063</S>, perform<S>7999</S> thy vows<S>5088</S>; for the wicked<S>1100</S> one shall no more<S>3254</S> pass through<S>5674</S> thee<S>3772</S>; he is utterly cut off.

He that dasheth in pieces<S>6327</S> is come up<S>5927</S> against thee<S>6440</S>: keep<S>5341</S> the fortress<S>4694</S>, watch<S>6822</S> the way<S>1870</S>, make thy loins<S>4975</S> strong<S>2388</S>, fortify<S>553</S> thy power<S>3581</S> mightily<S>3966</S>.

For Jehovah<S>3068</S> restoreth<S>7725</S> the excellency<S>1347</S> of Jacob<S>3290</S>, as the excellency<S>1347</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>; for the emptiers<S>1238</S> have emptied them out<S>1238</S>, and destroyed<S>7843</S> their vine-branches<S>2156</S>.

The shield<S>4043</S> of his mighty men<S>1368</S> is made red<S>119</S>, the valiant<S>2428</S> men<S>582</S> are in scarlet<S>8529</S>: the chariots<S>7393</S> flash<S>784</S> with steel<S>6393</S> in the day<S>3117</S> of his preparation<S>3559</S>, and the cypress spears<S>1265</S> are brandished<S>7477</S>.

The chariots<S>7393</S> rage<S>1984</S> in the streets<S>2351</S>; they rush<S>8264</S> to and fro in the broad ways<S>7339</S>: the appearance<S>4758</S> of them is like torches<S>3940</S>; they run<S>7323</S> like the lightnings<S>1300</S>.

He remembereth<S>2142</S> his nobles<S>117</S>: they stumble<S>3782</S> in their march<S>1979</S>; they make haste<S>4116</S> to the wall<S>2346</S> thereof<S>5526</S>, and the mantelet is prepared<S>3559</S>.
The gates<S>8179</S> of the rivers<S>5104</S> are opened<S>6605</S>, and the palace<S>1964</S> is dissolved<S>4127</S>.

And it is decreed<S>5324</S>: she is uncovered<S>1540</S>, she is carried away<S>5927</S>; and her handmaids<S>519</S> moan<S>5090</S> as with the voice<S>6963</S> of doves<S>3123</S>, beating<S>8608</S> upon their breasts<S>3824</S>.

But Nineveh<S>5210</S> hath been from of old<S>3117</S> like a pool<S>1295</S> of water<S>4325</S>: yet they flee away<S>5127</S>. Stand<S>5975</S>, stand<S>5975</S>, they cry<S>6437</S>; but none looketh back.

Take ye the spoil<S>962</S> of silver<S>3701</S>, take the spoil<S>962</S> of gold<S>2091</S>; for there is no end<S>7097</S> of the store<S>8498</S>, the glory<S>3519</S> of all goodly<S>2532</S> furniture<S>3627</S>.

She is empty<S>950</S>, and void<S>4003</S>, and waste<S>1110</S>; and the heart<S>3820</S> melteth<S>4549</S>, and the knees<S>1290</S> smite together<S>6375</S>, and anguish<S>2479</S> is in all loins<S>4975</S>, and the faces<S>6440</S> of them all are waxed<S>6908</S> pale<S>6289</S>.

Where is the den<S>4583</S> of the lions<S>738</S>, and the feeding-place<S>4829</S> of the young lions<S>3715</S>, where the lion<S>738</S> and the lioness<S>738</S> walked<S>1980</S>, the lion<S>3833</S>'s whelp<S>1482</S>, and none made them afraid<S>2729</S>?

The lion<S>738</S> did tear in pieces<S>2963</S> enough<S>1767</S> for his whelps<S>1484</S>, and strangled<S>2614</S> for his lionesses<S>3833</S>, and filled<S>4390</S> his caves<S>2356</S> with prey<S>2964</S>, and his dens<S>4585</S> with ravin<S>2966</S>.

Behold<S>5002</S>, I am against thee, saith Jehovah<S>3068</S> of hosts<S>6635</S>, and I will burn<S>1197</S> her chariots<S>7393</S> in the smoke<S>6227</S>, and the sword<S>2719</S> shall devour<S>398</S> thy young lions<S>3715</S>; and I will cut off<S>3772</S> thy prey<S>2964</S> from the earth<S>776</S>, and the voice<S>6963</S> of thy messengers<S>4397</S> shall no more be heard<S>8085</S>.
Woe<S>1945</S> to the bloody<S>1818</S> city<S>5892</S>! it is all full<S>4392</S> of lies<S>3585</S> and rapine<S>6563</S>; the prey<S>2964</S> departeth not<S>4185</S>.

The noise<S>6963</S> of the whip<S>7752</S>, and the noise<S>6963</S> of the rattling<S>7494</S> of wheels<S>212</S>, and prancing<S>1725</S> horses<S>5483</S>, and bounding<S>7540</S> chariots<S>4818</S>,

the horseman<S>6571</S> mounting<S>5927</S>, and the flashing<S>1300</S> sword<S>2719</S>, and the glittering<S>1300</S> spear<S>2595</S>, and a multitude<S>7230</S> of slain<S>2491</S>, and a great<S>3514</S> heap of corpses<S>6297</S>, and there is no end<S>7097</S> of the bodies<S>6297</S>; they stumble<S>3782</S> upon their bodies<S>1472</S>;—

because of the multitude<S>7230</S> of the whoredoms<S>2183</S> of the well-favored<S>2896</S> harlot<S>2181</S>, the mistress<S>1172</S> of witchcrafts<S>3785</S>, that selleth<S>4376</S> nations<S>1471</S> through her whoredoms<S>2183</S>, and families<S>4940</S> through her witchcrafts<S>3785</S>.

Behold<S>5002</S>, I am against thee, saith Jehovah<S>3068</S> of hosts<S>6635</S>, and I will uncover<S>1540</S> thy skirts<S>7757</S> upon thy face<S>6440</S>; and I will show<S>7200</S> the nations<S>1471</S> thy nakedness<S>4626</S>, and the kingdoms<S>4467</S> thy shame<S>7036</S>.
And I will cast<S>7993</S> abominable filth<S>8251</S> upon thee<S>5034</S>, and make thee vile, and will set<S>7760</S> thee as a gazing-stock<S>7210</S>.

And it shall come to pass<S>7200</S>, that all they that look upon thee shall flee<S>5074</S> from thee<S>559</S>, and say, Nineveh<S>5210</S> is laid waste<S>7703</S>: who will bemoan<S>5110</S> her<S>370</S>? whence shall I seek<S>1245</S> comforters for thee<S>5162</S>?

Art thou better<S>3190</S> than No<S>4996</S> - amon<S>527</S>, that was situate<S>3427</S> among the rivers<S>2975</S>, that had the waters<S>4325</S> round about<S>5439</S> her<S>2426</S>; whose rampart was the sea<S>3220</S>, and her wall<S>2346</S> was of the sea<S>3220</S>?
Ethiopia<S>3568</S> and Egypt<S>4714</S> were her strength<S>6109</S>, and it was infinite<S>369</S>; Put<S>6316</S> and Lubim<S>3864</S> were thy helpers<S>5833</S>.

Yet was she carried away<S>1473</S>, she went<S>1980</S> into captivity<S>7628</S>; her young children<S>5768</S> also were dashed in pieces<S>7376</S> at the head<S>7218</S> of all the streets<S>2351</S>; and they cast<S>3032</S> lots<S>1486</S> for her honorable men<S>3513</S>, and all her great men<S>1419</S> were bound<S>7576</S> in chains<S>2131</S>.
Thou also shalt be drunken<S>7937</S>; thou shalt be hid<S>5956</S>; thou also shalt seek<S>1245</S> a stronghold<S>4581</S> because of the enemy<S>341</S>.

All thy fortresses<S>4013</S> shall be like fig-trees<S>8384</S> with the first-ripe<S>1061</S> figs: if they be shaken<S>5128</S>, they fall<S>5307</S> into the mouth<S>6310</S> of the eater<S>398</S>.

Behold<S>5971</S>, thy people in the midst<S>7130</S> of thee are women<S>802</S>; the gates<S>8179</S> of thy land<S>776</S> are set wide<S>6605</S> open<S>6605</S> unto thine enemies<S>341</S>: the fire<S>784</S> hath devoured<S>398</S> thy bars<S>1280</S>.

Draw<S>7579</S> thee water<S>4325</S> for the siege<S>4692</S>; strengthen<S>2388</S> thy fortresses<S>4013</S>; go<S>935</S> into the clay<S>2916</S>, and tread<S>7429</S> the mortar<S>2563</S>; make strong<S>2388</S> the brickkiln<S>4404</S>.

There shall the fire<S>784</S> devour<S>398</S> thee<S>2719</S>; the sword shall cut thee off<S>3772</S>; it shall devour<S>398</S> thee like the canker-worm<S>3218</S>: make thyself many<S>3513</S> as the canker-worm<S>3218</S>; make thyself many<S>3513</S> as the locust<S>697</S>.
Thou hast multiplied<S>7235</S> thy merchants<S>7402</S> above the stars<S>3556</S> of heaven<S>8064</S>: the canker-worm<S>3218</S> ravageth<S>6584</S>, and fleeth away<S>5774</S>.

Thy princes<S>4502</S> are as the locusts<S>697</S>, and thy marshals<S>2951</S> as the swarms of grasshoppers<S>1462</S>, which encamp<S>2583</S> in the hedges<S>1448</S> in the cold<S>7135</S> day<S>3117</S>, but when the sun<S>8121</S> ariseth<S>2224</S> they flee away<S>5074</S>, and their place<S>4725</S> is not known<S>3045</S> where they are<S>335</S>.

Thy shepherds<S>7462</S> slumber<S>5123</S>, O king<S>4428</S> of Assyria<S>804</S>; thy nobles<S>117</S> are at rest<S>7931</S>; thy people<S>5971</S> are scattered<S>6335</S> upon the mountains<S>2022</S>, and there is none to gather them<S>6908</S>.

There is no assuaging<S>3545</S> of thy hurt<S>7667</S>: thy wound<S>4347</S> is grievous<S>2470</S>: all that hear<S>8085</S> the report<S>8088</S> of thee clap<S>8628</S> their hands<S>3709</S> over thee<S>7451</S>; for upon whom hath not thy wickedness passed<S>5674</S> continually<S>8548</S>?
